summary:
Conducted comprehensive security and code quality audit of radar/scraping/ and radar/scraper/ modules. Identified 24 issues across 5 high, 9 medium, and 10 low severity categories.

HIGH SEVERITY (5):
- datetime.utcnow() deprecated in Python 3.12+ found in reddit.py:113, hackernews.py:91, devto.py:99, lobsters.py:104
- PRAW Reddit client missing timeout configuration at reddit.py:49

MEDIUM SEVERITY (9):
- SafeHTTPClient hardcoded retry parameters (3, 2, 30) override init params at http.py:122
- Missing JSON decode error handling in hackernews.py:48, devto.py:53, lobsters.py:54
- Hardcoded Reddit post limit (25) not configurable at reddit.py:59
- Broad exception handling in base.py:51 masks specific error types
- DNS failure silently ignored in scraper/base.py:58
- Generic exception in scraper/base.py:79 hides actual failures
- Broad exception catch in scraper/base.py:86 swallows errors silently

LOW SEVERITY (10):
- DNS resolution not cached in http.py:109
- Empty except at reddit.py:86 without specific error logging
- Hardcoded HN tags at hackernews.py:17
- Hardcoded Dev.to tags at devto.py:17
- Hardcoded Dev.to pagination params at devto.py:50
- Hardcoded Lobsters endpoints at lobsters.py:16
- int() conversions in base.py:86 may raise ValueError
- Exponential backoff in scraper/base.py:89 unbounded
- Empty list return in scraper/base.py:90 masks failure state

RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Replace all datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc)
- Add timeout parameter to PRAW instantiation
- Use instance retry params in SafeHTTPClient decorators
- Add try-except JSONDecodeError wrappers for all .json() calls
- Make all hardcoded constants configurable via Settings
- Add specific exception type handlers with logging
- Implement DNS resolution cache in SafeHTTPClient
- Cap exponential backoff and make configurable

Tests passed: 217/217 (all existing tests remain green)
